Motion For Mandatory Or Discretionary Relief Per Ccp 473b From Order Striking Reply To Plaintiffs Opposition To Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ariel M. Gershon, Esq., Greenstein & McDonald Attorney at Law, Telephone 415-773-1240, appearing for Plaintiff;
No other appearance.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS RENKA PROP LLC, JULIA MWANGI, and ANNE KIHAGI'S Motion For Mandatory Or Discretionary Relief From Order Striking Reply To Plaintiffs Opposition To Motion For Summary Judgment, Per Section 473b Code of Civil Procedure

GRANTED in part. The Court will consider Defendants' late-filed reply brief, but will not consider the voluminous additional documentary evidence improperly included with that brief, which Defendants' counsel's declaration fails to explain or justify. "The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers. This principle is most prominent in the context of summary judgment motions, which is not surprising, given that it is a common evidentiary motion. '[T]he inclusion of additional evidentiary matter with the reply should only be allowed in the exceptional case . . . . .'" (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 .) The Court denies Defendants' request to schedule a second hearing on Defendants' motion for summary judgment. The Court has considered the late-filed reply brief, which does not alter the Court's view of the merits of Defendants' motion for summary judgment or alternatively for summary adjudication. The Court will not reconsider its March 19, 2019 order denying Defendants' motion. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions in their opposition brief is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Amended Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ariel Gershon, Esq. 415-773-1240 x351 for Plaintiff.
Karen Uchiyama, Esq. 415-563-9300 for Defendants Anne Kihagi, Renka Prop, LLC and Julia Mwangi.

RULING - A R G U E D. The court shares it's views, it is clear the moving party is not entitled to judgment on the pleadings. Defendant argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Anne Kihagi, Renka Prop, LLC and Julia Mwangi's Amended Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Anne Kihagi, Renka Prop, LLC, and Julia Mwangi's motion for judgment on the pleadings on both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs James Austin and Raymond Schreiber is denied as to both causes of action. The motion is an impermissible motion for reconsideration of the March 12, 2018 order denying defendants' first motion for judgment on the pleadings. The request to take judicial notice of Ms. Mwangi's declarations is denied since the content of those declarations is subject to dispute by the parties. Plaintiffs allege sufficient ultimate facts to support both of their causes of action. The complaint alleges that plaintiffs discovered that Ms. Mwangi had left the apartment December 2016, which is sufficient to defeat this motion to the extent that it is based on the one-year statute of limitations. While the request to take judicial notice of Mr. Austin's testimony in CCSF v. Kihagi is granted, that testimony does not establish that either of the plaintiffs' claims lack merit as a matter of law.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Matter passed for counsel to discuss among themselves whether they disagree with the Court's revised tentative or to see if they can reach some agreement. If not, counsel will return to to court to argue against the revised tentative.

Mr. Gershon addressed the statement by Ms. Uchiyama in her declaration with regard to serving late discovery responses to plaintiffs on October 12th. Argument held.
